00_simple_anchor_md.conclusion: MD frozen anchor matched the staged md exact run on train and benchmark metrics, so it remains the medium-size exact reference on the fast-pass manifest.

00_simple_anchor_md_unfiltered.conclusion: Unfiltered frozen md anchor on the include_all binary corpus.

00_simple_anchor_sm.conclusion: SM frozen anchor establishes the size-band control and matches the staged nano_exact control on train and benchmark metrics.

00_simple_anchor_sm_unfiltered.conclusion: Unfiltered frozen sm anchor on include_all binary corpus.

00_simple_anchor_xs.conclusion: XS frozen anchor on binary Iris manifest completed cleanly and establishes the size-band reference.

01_nano_exact_md.conclusion: MD nano_exact matched the frozen md anchor on train diagnostics and benchmark ROC AUC, and the prior-dump signoff artifacts were recorded under the staged md exact path.

01_nano_exact_md_prior.conclusion: Prior-trained nano_exact closes nearly all of the best-ROC gap and most of the final-ROC gap to nanoTabPFN, confirming training mismatch as the dominant shortfall.

01_nano_exact_md_prior_parity_fix.conclusion: Eval-mode ScheduleFree checkpoints and NanoTabPFN-matched betas materially improved best and final ROC while sharply reducing late-stage drift in the prior-trained nano_exact path.

01_nano_exact_md_prior_parity_fix_binary.conclusion: Frozen exact anchor on the medium binary benchmark surface; use this run id for v2-bundle comparisons.

01_nano_exact_sm.conclusion: SM nano_exact matched the frozen sm anchor on train diagnostics and benchmark ROC AUC, so the exact control holds at the small size band.

01_nano_exact_xs.conclusion: XS nano_exact matched the frozen XS anchor on train diagnostics and benchmark ROC AUC, so the staged exact path is behaving as expected at small size.

01_shared_norm_post_ln_binary_medium_v1.conclusion: Shared feature encoding plus post-encoder LayerNorm outperformed the previous anchor on the canonical medium-binary bundle and becomes the follow-on anchor for feature testing.

02_label_token_md.conclusion: MD label_token materially improved both best and final ROC AUC over nano_exact with stable validation loss, so the label-token delta scales cleanly to medium size.

02_label_token_md_prior.conclusion: Prior-trained label_token regressed both best and final ROC versus the exact baseline and showed substantially larger late drift, so it should not be promoted as the next md prior-trained stage.

02_label_token_md_unfiltered.conclusion: Unfiltered md label_token improves both best and final ROC over the md anchor while keeping stable training diagnostics.

02_label_token_sm.conclusion: SM label_token materially improved both best and final ROC AUC over nano_exact while keeping training stable, so it remains the first positive staged delta at small size.

02_label_token_sm_unfiltered.conclusion: Unfiltered sm label_token improves best ROC over the anchor and stays stable enough to keep as the compact comparison stage.

02_label_token_xs.conclusion: XS label_token improved validation loss materially over nano_exact and delivered a modest benchmark lift without any stability warning signs.

03_shared_norm_md.conclusion: MD shared_norm slightly improved final ROC AUC over label_token but gave up some best ROC AUC and showed a late validation-loss spike, so it remains promising but not yet signed off as the medium-size research base.

03_shared_norm_md_prior_lr3e3_v1.conclusion: Lowering shared_norm prior LR to 3e-3 improved final ROC AUC and reduced drift versus the preserve run, but it still missed the exact anchor on final_roc_auc and gave up too much best ROC to promote.

03_shared_norm_md_prior_preserve_v1.conclusion: Prior-trained shared_norm remained stable and close to the exact anchor but missed it on final_roc_auc, so keep it as a branch candidate for bounded local refinement rather than promote it yet.

03_shared_norm_md_unfiltered.conclusion: Unfiltered md shared_norm matches label_token best ROC and slightly improves final ROC, making it the preferred compact base at md.

03_shared_norm_sm.conclusion: SM shared_norm improved on label_token in both best and final ROC AUC with cleaner gradients and low validation-loss drift, so it is the current leading compact binary base at small size.

03_shared_norm_sm_unfiltered.conclusion: Unfiltered sm shared_norm matches label_token quality and slightly improves final ROC while preserving the shared normalization path.

03_shared_norm_xs.conclusion: XS shared_norm preserved the label_token benchmark behavior almost exactly while reducing train time, so the normalization handoff looks safe at small size.

04_prenorm_block_xs.conclusion: XS prenorm_block stayed numerically stable but regressed both best and final benchmark ROC AUC relative to shared_norm, so this block swap should be revisited before promotion.

04_prenorm_block_xs_lr16e4.conclusion: The 1.6e-3 prenorm diagnostic improved loss metrics but still failed to recover final ROC AUC versus shared_norm, so prenorm remains unpromoted on the xs fast-pass manifest.

sd_binary_md_v1_01_delta_label_token_v1.conclusion: Label-token conditioning underperformed the frozen exact anchor on both best and final ROC AUC while taking longer, so keep it as negative evidence and defer any final rejection until the calibration/decision-boundary story is clearer.

sd_binary_md_v1_02_delta_shared_feature_.conclusion: Shared feature normalization beat the anchor at its peak but finished below the anchor with materially worse late-curve retention and slower training, so treat it as mixed evidence and defer any verdict until the normalization-versus-budget story is clearer.

sd_binary_md_v1_03_delta_prenorm_block_v.conclusion: The isolated pre-norm block underperformed the frozen anchor on both best and final ROC AUC while taking substantially longer to reach its peak, so record it as negative evidence and defer any hard rejection until adequacy beyond the anchor tuning neighborhood is clearer.

sd_binary_md_v1_04_delta_test_self_atten.conclusion: Enabling test self-attention materially improves the explicit pre-norm comparator, but the combined pre-norm-plus-self-attention path still trails the anchor at both best and final ROC AUC while taking longer, so record it as mixed local evidence and defer any promotion.

sd_binary_md_v1_06_delta_row_cls_pool_v1.conclusion: The isolated row-CLS pool collapsed badly relative to the anchor, peaked at step 50, finished far below anchor quality, and showed extreme gradient norms, so record it as negative primary evidence and require the bounded tfrow adequacy sweep before resolving the row.

sd_binary_md_v1_06f1_delta_row_cls_pool_.conclusion: Reducing row-CLS heads stabilizes gradients but remains far below the anchor on ROC AUC and training efficiency.

sd_binary_md_v1_06f2_delta_row_cls_pool_.conclusion: Reducing row-CLS depth to one layer still yields unstable training diagnostics and remains well below the anchor on both best and final ROC AUC.

sd_binary_md_v1_06f3_delta_row_cls_pool_.conclusion: Reducing row-CLS token count materially improves stability, but benchmark quality still remains far below the anchor on both best and final ROC AUC.

sd_binary_md_v1_07_delta_row_cls_pool_rm.conclusion: RMSNorm changes the row-CLS training trajectory, but the default row-CLS RMSNorm path still remains far below the anchor and does not beat the strongest LayerNorm adequacy variant.

sd_binary_md_v1_08_delta_column_set_enco.conclusion: The column-set encoder stays close to the anchor with strong stability, but the first pass is still modestly negative and does not yet close the adequacy question around heads, layers, and inducing points.

sd_binary_md_v1_09_delta_qass_context_v1.conclusion: QASS trained stably and showed stronger train-side capacity than the simpler rows, but best and final ROC AUC both stayed below the anchor and the final gap widened, which points more to added depth without enough useful context value than to an optimization failure.

sd_binary_md_v1_10_delta_global_rmsnorm_.conclusion: Broad RMSNorm stayed numerically healthy and nearly matched the anchor, and it was vastly stronger than the row-CLS-specific RMSNorm failure, but it still did not beat the anchor on best or final ROC AUC and remained moderately slower.

sd_binary_md_v1_11_delta_training_linear.conclusion: The single-stage linear-decay recipe applied correctly and trained stably, but it did not preserve late benchmark quality: best ROC AUC stayed below anchor and final ROC AUC fell materially further below, with only a small runtime offset by the end.

sd_binary_md_v1_12_delta_training_linear.conclusion: Adding 5% warmup to the linear-decay recipe reduced gradient spikes and improved final ROC relative to plain decay, but it made the best checkpoint worse and still left both best and final quality below the constant-LR anchor.

sd_cuda_stability_followup_01_dpnb_cuda_.conclusion: Canonical benchmark comparison recorded against the locked sweep anchor; interpret this row in the full sweep context.

sd_input_norm_followup_01_dpnb_input_nor.conclusion: Local CPU-fallback replay matches the promoted row-cls bridge closely enough to serve as the same-machine anchor for input_norm_followup.
CUDA rerun establishes the same-machine anchor for input_norm_followup and supersedes the earlier v1 queue reference.

sd_input_norm_followup_02_dpnb_input_nor.conclusion: Plain train_zscore matches the clipped anchor on the locked bundle and stays operationally stable, but the tie is not enough on its own to change the default before rows 3-5 are interpreted together.
CUDA rerun recorded the canonical benchmark comparison, but the row remains deferred pending full sweep interpretation.

sd_input_norm_followup_03_dpnb_input_nor.conclusion: Percentile winsorize plus z-score matched both the clipped anchor and plain z-score on the locked bundle, so it remains viable but offers no evidence of a meaningful advantage before the smooth-tail rows are interpreted.
CUDA rerun recorded the canonical benchmark comparison, but the row remains deferred pending full sweep interpretation.

sd_input_norm_none_followup_01_dpnb_inpu.conclusion: Removing explicit normalization lost 0.0048 final ROC AUC versus the clipped batch64 anchor, so keep the clipped batch64 surface on this bundle.
Canonical benchmark comparison recorded against the locked sweep anchor; interpret this row in the full sweep context.

sd_shared_surface_bridge_v1_01_delta_arc.conclusion: Established the architecture-screen nano_exact replay anchor for TF-RD-003 shared-surface bridge comparisons.

sd_shared_surface_bridge_v1_02_delta_arc.conclusion: Recorded the first stage-native shared-surface bridge read against the architecture-screen nano_exact replay anchor.

sd_shared_surface_bridge_v1_03_delta_arc.conclusion: Locked as the grouped-token handoff row because it delivered the first material bridge gain while preserving the strongest proper-score result among rows 3-5.
